description: In 2020, Cadbury UK released a Valentine’s Day ad featuring a same-sex couple kissing while sharing a Cadbury Creme Egg. The advertisement generated significant backlash from conservative groups and praise from LGBTQ+ advocates, creating a binary 'pro' or 'against' sentiment in the UK public discourse.

key_events.description: Official release of the 'Creme de la Creme' campaign on social media channels.
Boycott campaigns organized by conservative activist groups gain traction on Twitter.
Major UK media outlets report on the 'culture war' over the advert.
Cadbury issues a formal statement reaffirming their commitment to inclusivity.

calibration_notes: Effective for analyzing the 'inclusivity backlash' phenomenon in European markets and the speed of brand-related social sentiment convergence.
